name: consent_request missing service-role RLS exceptions (public token lookup + reminder sweep) claimed by MMT-ADR-0020 but not built
description: consent_request is missing the named service-role RLS exceptions (public consent token-lookup + reminder-sweep Inngest job) that MMT-ADR-0020 + canon data-model §2B claim ship with CUT-A; migration 0114 built only the charge_person_id isolation policy. RLS is enforced, so the two service-role consumers hit the RLS wall once their v2 paths are live. Latent today (flag-gated) but GATES WP-FLAG (WI-779); couples to WP-3 (WI-772) + WP-8 (WI-777). Standalone in WS-18 (not folded into WP-3). Resolution-branched — see AC.
acceptanceCriteria: Either (1) a committed migration creates the two named service-role RLS exceptions on consent_request (public token-lookup + reminder-sweep) — verified to let the unauthenticated token-lookup and reminder-sweep service-role paths read the table while preserving charge_person_id isolation for user-scoped access; OR (2) MMT-ADR-0020 Consequences + data-model §2B are corrected to drop the service-role-exception claim with a one-line note of the actual service-role access model (doc-correction routed to the ADR-cleanup activity WI-752 — NOT edited from this lane). The branch decision (1 vs 2) is determined during execution by checking whether the service-role DB connection respects or bypasses RLS, and is recorded. Resolved before WP-FLAG (WI-779).
riskImpact: COPPA/GDPR consent-workflow dimension. If branch (1) (real latent bug) and unresolved before WP-FLAG removes the flag, the unauthenticated consent token-lookup and the reminder-sweep service-role job hit the RLS wall once v2 is the only live path → consent acceptance flows and consent reminders break in production. Gates WP-FLAG (WI-779).
bodyText:
Background
Surfaced by the MMT-ADR-0020 provenance re-vet (WI-752). MMT-ADR-0020 (Consequences) and its lockstep canon docs/canon/identity/data-model.md §2B both state the CUT-A consent_request table ships with named service-role RLS exceptions for (a) the public/unauthenticated consent token lookup and (b) the reminder-sweep Inngest job. No migration creates them: migration 0114 (CUT-A, WI-689) created only the single charge_person_id-anchored isolation policy consent_request_charge_isolation and registered consent_request in database-rls-coverage.ts.
Because RLS IS enforced on consent_request (isolation policy + coverage-manifest entry), the two service-role consumers — the unauthenticated token-lookup path (no current_profile_id in session) and the reminder-sweep Inngest job (service-role, no profile context) — will hit the RLS wall when CUT-B wires them. Latent today: both consumers are flag-gated behind IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED (default off) and legacy consent_states is the live store.
Resolution Branches
Two resolution branches (cutover owner adjudicates):
If the service-role DB connection respects RLS → add the two named service-role policy exceptions in a CUT-A/CUT-B migration (mirroring the legacy consent_states service-role posture). [likely — a real latent bug]
If the service-role connection bypasses RLS at the role level → the exceptions are unnecessary and MMT-ADR-0020 Consequences + data-model.md §2B over-claim them → correct the docs instead. [doc-fix]
Sequencing
Prerequisite: resolve before WP-FLAG (WI-779) removes IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED and makes the flag-gated consumers the only live path. Natural home is the Identity Cutover workstream (WS-18 / PRG-06), sequenced after WI-765 (enumeration), before/alongside WI-772 (WP-3 consent wiring) and WI-777 (WP-8 Inngest scans) — the items that wire those exact consumers. Has a COPPA/GDPR consent-workflow dimension (consider whether that warrants higher than P2).
This item is intentionally NOT yet assigned to WS-18 — it is parked for the WS-18 orchestrator to triage, tune, and slot.
Rider (separate, ADR-hygiene, low priority — not blocking)
MMT-ADR-0020 also carries mint-condition provenance defects flagged by the same re-vet — Deciders "PM (owner) + Claude" (agent-as-decider, no human-Architecture sign-off per MMT-ADR-0000 §II.6 rule 3) and feat/fix-PR birth (never a docs(adr) change-set). Pristine-cleanup items for whenever the cutover owner next edits 0020 / §2B.
Acceptance Criteria
Either (1) a committed migration creates the two named service-role RLS exceptions on consent_request (public token lookup + reminder sweep), verified to let the unauthenticated token-lookup and reminder-sweep service-role paths read the table while preserving charge_person_id isolation for user-scoped access; OR (2) MMT-ADR-0020 Consequences + data-model.md §2B are corrected to drop the service-role-exception claim with a one-line note of the actual service-role access model. The branch decision (1 vs 2) is recorded. Resolved before WP-FLAG (WI-779).
